title: Isha Upanishad
description: One of the shortest and most condensed of the principal Upanishads. 18 verses on the all-pervading nature of the Self, the relationship between knowledge and action, and the nature of liberation.
source: Sanskrit text public domain (Shukla Yajur Veda, c. 700 BCE). English translations are original paraphrases by the Divya Granth project, not derived from any copyrighted modern translation.

verses.sanskrit:
ॐ ईशा वास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्।
तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
oṃ īśā vāsyam idaṃ sarvaṃ yat kiñca jagatyāṃ jagat.
tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasyasvid dhanam.

translations.en: All this — whatever moves in this moving world — is enveloped by the Lord. Find your enjoyment in renunciation; do not covet what belongs to anyone.

verses.sanskrit:
कुर्वन्नेवेह कर्माणि जिजीविषेच्छतꣳ समाः।
एवं त्वयि नान्यथेतोऽस्ति न कर्म लिप्यते नरे॥
verses.transliteration:
kurvann eveha karmāṇi jijīviṣec chataṃ samāḥ.
evaṃ tvayi nānyatheto'sti na karma lipyate nare.

translations.en: Performing actions in this world, one should wish to live a hundred years. There is no other way for you; action does not bind a person who lives like this.

verses.sanskrit:
असुर्या नाम ते लोका अन्धेन तमसाऽऽवृताः।
ताꣳस्ते प्रेत्याभिगच्छन्ति ये के चात्महनो जनाः॥
verses.transliteration:
asuryā nāma te lokā andhena tamasā''vṛtāḥ.
tāṃs te pretyābhigacchanti ye ke cātmahano janāḥ.

translations.en: There are worlds called demonic, wrapped in blinding darkness. Those who slay the Self go there after death.

verses.sanskrit:
अनेजदेकं मनसो जवीयो नैनद्देवा आप्नुवन्पूर्वमर्षत्।
तद्धावतोऽन्यानत्येति तिष्ठत्तस्मिन्नपो मातरिश्वा दधाति॥
verses.transliteration:
anejad ekaṃ manaso javīyo nainad devā āpnuvan pūrvam arṣat.
tad dhāvato'nyān atyeti tiṣṭhat tasminn apo mātariśvā dadhāti.

translations.en: Unmoving, one, swifter than the mind — the senses cannot reach it; it has gone before. Standing still, it outruns all who run. In it the cosmic wind sets the waters in motion.

verses.sanskrit:
तदेजति तन्नैजति तद्दूरे तद्वन्तिके।
तदन्तरस्य सर्वस्य तदु सर्वस्यास्य बाह्यतः॥
verses.transliteration:
tad ejati tan naijati tad dūre tad v antike.
tad antarasya sarvasya tad u sarvasyāsya bāhyataḥ.

translations.en: It moves and it does not move. It is far and it is near. It is within all this, and it is outside all this.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्तु सर्वाणि भूतान्यात्मन्येवानुपश्यति।
सर्वभूतेषु चात्मानं ततो न विजुगुप्सते॥
verses.transliteration:
yas tu sarvāṇi bhūtāny ātmany evānupaśyati.
sarvabhūteṣu cātmānaṃ tato na vijugupsate.

translations.en: One who sees all beings in the Self alone, and the Self in all beings, no longer recoils from anything.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्मिन्सर्वाणि भूतान्यात्मैवाभूद्विजानतः।
तत्र को मोहः कः शोक एकत्वमनुपश्यतः॥
verses.transliteration:
yasmin sarvāṇi bhūtāny ātmaivābhūd vijānataḥ.
tatra ko mohaḥ kaḥ śoka ekatvam anupaśyataḥ.

translations.en: When all beings have become the Self for the one who knows — what delusion, what sorrow can there be for one who sees the unity?

verses.sanskrit:
स पर्यगाच्छुक्रमकायमव्रणमस्नाविरꣳ शुद्धमपापविद्धम्।
कविर्मनीषी परिभूः स्वयम्भूर्याथातथ्यतोऽर्थान्व्यदधाच्छाश्वतीभ्यः समाभ्यः॥
verses.transliteration:
sa paryagāc chukram akāyam avraṇam asnāviraṃ śuddham apāpaviddham.
kavir manīṣī paribhūḥ svayambhūr yāthātathyato'rthān vyadadhāc chāśvatībhyaḥ samābhyaḥ.

translations.en: He has reached out and pervaded everything — bright, bodiless, without wound, without sinews, pure, untouched by evil. The seer, the thinker, all-pervading, self-existent, has rightly distributed all things for endless years.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽविद्यामुपासते।
ततो भूय इव ते तमो य उ विद्यायाꣳ रताः॥
verses.transliteration:
andhaṃ tamaḥ praviśanti ye'vidyām upāsate.
tato bhūya iva te tamo ya u vidyāyāṃ ratāḥ.

translations.en: Into blinding darkness enter those who worship ignorance; into still greater darkness, those who delight in knowledge alone.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्यदेवाहुर्विद्ययाऽन्यदाहुरविद्यया।
इति शुश्रुम धीराणां ये नस्तद्विचचक्षिरे॥
verses.transliteration:
anyad evāhur vidyayā'nyad āhur avidyayā.
iti śuśruma dhīrāṇāṃ ye nas tad vicacakṣire.

translations.en: Different, they say, is the result of knowledge; different, they say, the result of ignorance. So we have heard from the wise who explained it to us.

verses.sanskrit:
विद्यां चाविद्यां च यस्तद्वेदोभयꣳ सह।
अविद्यया मृत्युं तीर्त्वा विद्ययाऽमृतमश्नुते॥
verses.transliteration:
vidyāṃ cāvidyāṃ ca yas tad vedobhayaṃ saha.
avidyayā mṛtyuṃ tīrtvā vidyayā'mṛtam aśnute.

translations.en: One who knows both knowledge and ignorance together — by ignorance crosses over death, and by knowledge attains immortality.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽसम्भूतिमुपासते।
ततो भूय इव ते तमो य उ सम्भूत्याꣳ रताः॥
verses.transliteration:
andhaṃ tamaḥ praviśanti ye'sambhūtim upāsate.
tato bhūya iva te tamo ya u sambhūtyāṃ ratāḥ.

translations.en: Into blinding darkness enter those who worship the unmanifest; into still greater darkness, those who delight in the manifest alone.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्यदेवाहुः सम्भवादन्यदाहुरसम्भवात्।
इति शुश्रुम धीराणां ये नस्तद्विचचक्षिरे॥
verses.transliteration:
anyad evāhuḥ sambhavād anyad āhur asambhavāt.
iti śuśruma dhīrāṇāṃ ye nas tad vicacakṣire.

translations.en: Different, they say, is the result of the manifest; different, the result of the unmanifest. So we have heard from the wise who explained it to us.

verses.sanskrit:
सम्भूतिं च विनाशं च यस्तद्वेदोभयꣳ सह।
विनाशेन मृत्युं तीर्त्वा सम्भूत्याऽमृतमश्नुते॥
verses.transliteration:
sambhūtiṃ ca vināśaṃ ca yas tad vedobhayaṃ saha.
vināśena mṛtyuṃ tīrtvā sambhūtyā'mṛtam aśnute.

translations.en: One who knows both the manifest and the perishable together — by the perishable crosses over death, and by the manifest attains immortality.

verses.sanskrit:
हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण सत्यस्यापिहितं मुखम्।
तत्त्वं पूषन्नपावृणु सत्यधर्माय दृष्टये॥
verses.transliteration:
hiraṇmayena pātreṇa satyasyāpihitaṃ mukham.
tat tvaṃ pūṣann apāvṛṇu satyadharmāya dṛṣṭaye.

translations.en: The face of truth is hidden by a golden disk. Uncover it, O Pushan, that I — devoted to truth — may see it.

verses.sanskrit:
पूषन्नेकर्षे यम सूर्य प्राजापत्य व्यूह रश्मीन्समूह तेजः।
यत्ते रूपं कल्याणतमं तत्ते पश्यामि योऽसावसौ पुरुषः सोऽहमस्मि॥
verses.transliteration:
pūṣann ekarṣe yama sūrya prājāpatya vyūha raśmīn samūha tejaḥ.
yat te rūpaṃ kalyāṇatamaṃ tat te paśyāmi yo'sāv asau puruṣaḥ so'ham asmi.

translations.en: O Pushan, sole seer, controller, sun, son of Prajapati — gather your rays, withdraw your blazing light. That most blessed form of yours, that I may see. The Person who dwells there — I am that.

verses.sanskrit:
वायुरनिलममृतमथेदं भस्मान्तꣳ शरीरम्।
ॐ क्रतो स्मर कृतꣳ स्मर क्रतो स्मर कृतꣳ स्मर॥
verses.transliteration:
vāyur anilam amṛtam athedaṃ bhasmāntaṃ śarīram.
oṃ krato smara kṛtaṃ smara krato smara kṛtaṃ smara.

translations.en: Let this breath return to the immortal wind; let this body end in ashes. Om — O mind, remember; remember what was done. O mind, remember; remember what was done.

verses.sanskrit:
अग्ने नय सुपथा राये अस्मान्विश्वानि देव वयुनानि विद्वान्।
युयोध्यस्मज्जुहुराणमेनो भूयिष्ठां ते नमउक्तिं विधेम॥
verses.transliteration:
agne naya supathā rāye asmān viśvāni deva vayunāni vidvān.
yuyodhy asmaj juhurāṇam eno bhūyiṣṭhāṃ te nama-uktiṃ vidhema.

translations.en: O Agni, lead us by the good path to prosperity, O God who knows all our deeds. Take from us our deceitful sins. To you we offer abundant words of homage.
